title: Mixed Graph: The charts below give information on the location and types of dance classes you...
description: The charts below give information on the location and types of dance classes young people in a town in Australia are currently attending. Su
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1 – PIE \+ BAR (DANCE CLASSES)

1. Phân tích nhanh

  • Pie chart: địa điểm lớp học
  • Bar chart: loại lớp theo độ tuổi
  • Đơn vị: % \+ number of students

→ Dạng mixed chart (composition \+ comparison)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

The pie chart illustrates the locations where dance classes are held, while the bar chart compares the number of students in different types of dance classes by age group.

3. Overview (quan trọng)

  • Private studios là địa điểm phổ biến nhất
  • College-based studios là ít nhất
  • Under 11 thích ballet và tap
  • 11–16 tuổi thích modern dance

→ Sự khác biệt rõ ràng theo độ tuổi

4. BODY 1 – Pie chart (location)

Private studios: 48% (largest)

School halls: 24%

Community halls: 18%

College-based: 10% (smallest)

→ Insight: phần lớn lớp học diễn ra ở private venues

5. BODY 2 – Bar chart (types by age)

Under 11:

  • Ballet: 600 (highest)
  • Tap: 500
  • Modern: 300 (lowest)

11–16:

  • Modern: 550 (highest)
  • Tap: 450
  • Ballet: 300 (lowest)

→ Insight: preference shifts from ballet → modern

6. Từ vựng nên dùng

account for

make up the largest proportion

be the most/least popular

there is a clear preference for

in contrast

by comparison

The pie chart shows the locations where dance classes are held, while the bar chart compares the number of students in three types of dance classes by age group.

Overall, private studios are the most common location for dance classes, and preferences for dance types differ notably between younger children and teenagers.

In terms of location, nearly half of all classes take place in private studios (48%). School halls (after-school) account for 24%, while community halls and other venues make up 18%. College-based studios are the least common, representing just 10% of the total.

Regarding types of classes, ballet is the most popular among children under 11, with 600 students, compared to only 300 in the 11–16 age group. Tap dancing also attracts a high number of younger students (500), slightly more than older participants (450). In contrast, modern dance is far more popular among teenagers, with 550 students, almost double the figure for younger children (300).

Overall, younger children tend to prefer more traditional styles such as ballet and tap, whereas older students show a stronger preference for modern dance.
